analysis_tasks.objective: Determine the complete AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding encryption implementation in EncryptionUtil: IV source (static vs. SecureRandom vs. derived), key material origin, and cipher instantiation pattern.
analysis_tasks.scope: fineract-core/src/main/java/org/apache/fineract/infrastructure/security/utils/EncryptionUtil.java — full class body, focusing on encrypt() / decrypt() methods and IV generation.
analysis_tasks.rationale: SIG-001 confirms AES/CBC usage. IV source is critical for security posture assessment. Agent 1 notes SecureRandom expected but unconfirmed. Full method body required per prompt rule (AES/CBC/IvParameterSpec keywords present).

analysis_tasks.objective: Determine PBKDF2WithHmacSHA1 key derivation parameters: iteration count, salt source, key length, and whether HMAC-SHA1 base is acceptable for the current threat model.
analysis_tasks.rationale: SIG-002 confirms PBKDF2/HMAC-SHA1 KDF. SHA1-based PBKDF2 has reduced quantum security margin. Iteration count and salt source must be confirmed before labeling.

analysis_tasks.objective: Confirm BCryptPasswordEncoder usage in DefaultPlatformPasswordEncoder: BCrypt work factor (cost parameter), whether a custom strength is configured, and whether the encoder is the sole password hashing mechanism.
analysis_tasks.rationale: SIG-003 confirms BCrypt. Work factor is security-critical and must be explicitly confirmed from source; Spring Security default is 10 but custom configuration is possible.

analysis_tasks.objective: Identify all concrete implementors of PlatformPasswordEncoder interface to determine the actual password hashing algorithm(s) in use at runtime.
analysis_tasks.scope: fineract-core/src/main/java/org/apache/fineract/infrastructure/security/service/PlatformPasswordEncoder.java — interface declaration; locate all implementing classes across the codebase.
analysis_tasks.rationale: SIG-004 flags this as interface-only — algorithm unknown. Without knowing all implementors, labeling is incomplete. DefaultPlatformPasswordEncoder (SIG-003) is the known candidate but others may exist.

analysis_tasks.objective: Determine whether DatabasePasswordEncryptor delegates correctly to EncryptionUtil and that no additional algorithm is introduced in the wrapper layer.
analysis_tasks.rationale: SIG-005 states delegation to EncryptionUtil. The wrapper layer must be inspected to confirm the AES/PBKDF2 chain holds and no secondary algorithm is introduced.

analysis_tasks.objective: Determine whether DatabasePasswordDecryptor delegates to EncryptionUtil symmetrically and that IV handling is consistent with the encryption path.
analysis_tasks.rationale: SIG-006 mirrors SIG-005. Decryption path must confirm IV extraction strategy (e.g., IV prepended to ciphertext) to allow complete labeling of the AES/CBC operation.

analysis_tasks.objective: Determine the JWT signature algorithm used in FineractJwtAuthenticationTokenConverter: RS256, HS256, or other; identify the key material (RSA public key, shared secret) and its source.
analysis_tasks.scope: fineract-security/src/main/java/org/apache/fineract/infrastructure/security/converter/FineractJwtAuthenticationTokenConverter.java — full class body including JWTParser.parse() call and any algorithm-specific configuration.
analysis_tasks.rationale: SIG-007 identifies JWT handling but algorithm is unknown. JWT signature algorithm is PROHIBITED from being inferred per prompt rules — must be confirmed from source or configuration.

analysis_tasks.objective: Determine whether UUIDAccessTokenGenerationService uses java.util.UUID (version 4 / random) or a cryptographically stronger generator, and whether SecureRandom is involved.
analysis_tasks.rationale: SIG-008 identifies UUID-based 2FA token generation. UUID.randomUUID() uses SecureRandom internally but this must be confirmed. Security classification depends on whether the token space is cryptographically random.

analysis_tasks.objective: Confirm that RandomOTPGenerator uses SecureRandom (not java.util.Random) and determine the OTP character space and length.
analysis_tasks.rationale: SIG-009 infers SecureRandom from class name only — Agent 1 explicitly flags this as needing verification. Using java.util.Random instead of SecureRandom would be a critical vulnerability.

analysis_tasks.objective: Inspect keystore.jks artifact: determine key type (RSA/EC/DSA), key size, certificate validity period, issuer (self-signed vs. CA), and intended usage (TLS, code signing, JWT).
analysis_tasks.scope: fineract-provider/src/main/resources/keystore.jks — binary artifact; must be examined via keytool or programmatic inspection.
analysis_tasks.rationale: SIG-010 identifies JKS keystore. Certificate parameters are unknown. Key size and algorithm determine quantum vulnerability. Self-signed vs. CA distinction affects trust boundary labeling.

analysis_tasks.objective: Determine the exact encryption operations performed by TenantPasswordEncryptionTask during the migration: which algorithm, which key, whether IV is stored alongside ciphertext.
analysis_tasks.rationale: SIG-011 identifies a historical plaintext-to-encrypted migration. Understanding the exact migration logic confirms whether the AES/PBKDF2 chain (EncryptionUtil) was applied consistently and whether IV was stored correctly.

analysis_tasks.objective: Inspect SecurityConfig for TLS protocol version constraints, cipher suite allowlist/denylist, and any HTTP security headers relevant to cryptographic configuration.
analysis_tasks.scope: fineract-provider/src/main/java/org/apache/fineract/infrastructure/core/config/SecurityConfig.java — full class body, focusing on HttpSecurity, SSL/TLS, and cipher suite configuration.
analysis_tasks.rationale: SIG-012 identifies the central Spring Security config. TLS version and cipher suite settings cannot be inferred from framework defaults — must be confirmed from source.

analysis_tasks.objective: Determine the 2FA OTP algorithm in TwoFactorServiceImpl: TOTP (RFC 6238) vs. HOTP (RFC 4226) vs. custom; identify the HMAC base (SHA1/SHA256/SHA512) and time step if TOTP.
analysis_tasks.scope: fineract-security/src/main/java/org/apache/fineract/infrastructure/security/service/TwoFactorServiceImpl.java — full class body and any TOTP/HOTP library dependencies.
analysis_tasks.entry_point: TwoFactorServiceImpl class — OTP generation and validation methods
analysis_tasks.rationale: SIG-013 flags OTP algorithm as unknown. TOTP/HOTP with SHA1 has different security properties than SHA256/SHA512. Algorithm is PROHIBITED from inference — must be confirmed from source.

analysis_tasks.objective: Inspect the Liquibase changelog SQL in 0007_encrypt_existing_tenant_passwords.xml to determine which SQL operations are used, whether encryption logic is embedded in SQL or calls a Java stored procedure, and which fields are encrypted.
analysis_tasks.rationale: SIG-014 is configuration_file evidence. The changelog may embed encryption logic directly or invoke TenantPasswordEncryptionTask. This disambiguation is required before labeling.

analysis_tasks.objective: Determine whether SpringSecurityPlatformSecurityContext contains any cryptographic operations (token validation, signature verification) or is purely an authorization context boundary.
analysis_tasks.rationale: SIG-015 marks this as a security context abstraction with 'not direct crypto usage' but requires verification. Must confirm classification before labeling as not_crypto.

analysis_tasks.objective: Inspect AuthorizationServerConfig to determine OAuth2 grant types, token endpoint security, and whether JWT signing keys are configured here (RS256 key pair source).
analysis_tasks.rationale: OAuth2 authorization server configuration may contain the JWT signing algorithm and key source that AT-007 is looking for. This is a high-value follow-up target for the JWT signature gap.

analysis_tasks.objective: Determine whether HashingService uses a cryptographic hash function (SHA-256, MD5) or a non-cryptographic hash, and identify the use case.
analysis_tasks.rationale: MessageDigest import in preliminary_imports implies SHA-family or MD5 usage. HashingService is a named wrapper — must determine algorithm and use case (integrity check, ID generation, etc.).

analysis_tasks.objective: Inspect TrustModifier to determine whether it disables SSL/TLS certificate verification (trust-all pattern) and the scope of its usage in the application.
analysis_tasks.rationale: Class name 'TrustModifier' in a security-sensitive financial application is a strong indicator of SSL verification bypass. This is a critical negative security finding if confirmed.

analysis_tasks.objective: Determine whether FineractClient (OkHttp-based) disables SSL certificate verification, uses a custom TrustManager, or applies a custom HostnameVerifier.
analysis_tasks.scope: fineract-client/src/main/java/org/apache/fineract/client/util/FineractClient.java — full class body, focusing on OkHttpClient builder configuration.
analysis_tasks.rationale: OkHttp client in a financial platform may disable SSL verification for test/integration convenience. This is a critical security-relevant finding. OkHttp3Config in fineract-provider may also be relevant.

analysis_tasks.objective: Inspect application.properties for any cryptographic configuration entries: TLS settings, key store paths, cipher suite properties, JWT issuer/algorithm declarations.
analysis_tasks.rationale: Spring Boot application.properties is the primary location for externalized TLS/security configuration. JWT algorithm and keystore path may be declared here rather than in code.

analysis_tasks.objective: Inspect OkHttp3Config bean definition to determine whether OkHttpClient is configured with a custom SSLSocketFactory, TrustManager, or HostnameVerifier.
analysis_tasks.rationale: Centralized HTTP client configuration may modify TLS trust behavior. Must confirm whether production configuration uses a hardened or relaxed trust model.

analysis_tasks.objective: Determine whether EncryptionUtil test class uses hardcoded test keys or reveals the expected key/IV structure used in production tests.
analysis_tasks.rationale: Test class for the primary encryption utility. Test vectors may reveal IV handling strategy, expected key format, and whether static test keys are used in production code paths.

analysis_tasks.objective: Classify LoanArrearsAgeingUpdateHandler: confirm that RSA references are purely domain acronym (Repayment Schedule Analysis) with no cryptographic usage.
analysis_tasks.rationale: FP-001 candidate. Agent 1 flags RSA as a domain acronym in this file. Must confirm no cryptographic javax.crypto or java.security imports are present.

analysis_tasks.objective: Classify ReprocessLoanTransactionsService: confirm that 'tls' occurrences are business terminology rather than transport security configuration.
analysis_tasks.rationale: FP-002 candidate. Must verify no TLS/SSL configuration imports or javax.net.ssl calls are present.

analysis_tasks.objective: Classify PasswordValidationPolicy: confirm that password policy enforcement (length, complexity) contains no cryptographic hashing or encoding operations.
analysis_tasks.rationale: FP-003 candidate. Password policy rules are not cryptographic operations. Must confirm absence of PasswordEncoder or MessageDigest usage.

analysis_tasks.objective: Classify GlAccountTypeMapper and PaymentTypeMapper: confirm that 'key' occurrences are Java Map keys or enum identifiers with no cryptographic key material.
analysis_tasks.rationale: FP-004 candidate. Must confirm these are data structure keys (e.g., Map.put(key, value)) with no javax.crypto.SecretKey or java.security.Key imports.

analysis_tasks.objective: Classify IdempotencyKeyGenerator and IdempotencyKeyResolver: determine whether 'key' is a business idempotency identifier (UUID/hash) or a cryptographic key, and whether any cryptographic primitive is used.
analysis_tasks.rationale: Negative candidate sweep. 'Key' in name but likely a business idempotency token. If UUID-based with no HMAC: not_crypto. If HMAC-based: crypto.

analysis_tasks.objective: Classify ExternalEventIdempotencyKeyGenerator: determine whether idempotency key generation uses a cryptographic hash (HMAC, SHA) or a non-cryptographic identifier.
analysis_tasks.rationale: Negative candidate sweep. External event idempotency keys may use MessageDigest (SHA-256) for deterministic key derivation — if so, this is a crypto finding. If UUID: not_crypto.

analysis_tasks.objective: Classify S3ContentStoreService: determine whether AWS S3 integration uses client-side encryption, server-side encryption configuration, or no encryption at the application layer.
analysis_tasks.entry_point: S3ContentStoreService class — S3 client construction and upload/download methods
analysis_tasks.rationale: Negative candidate sweep. S3 service may configure SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, or client-side encryption. Absence of encryption configuration is also a labeling outcome.

analysis_tasks.objective: Classify BasicAuthRequestInterceptor in fineract-client-feign: determine whether Basic Auth credentials are Base64-encoded only (standard) or additionally hashed/encrypted.
analysis_tasks.rationale: Negative candidate sweep. Basic Auth interceptors typically encode credentials in Base64 (not encryption). Must confirm no additional cryptographic transformation is applied.

evidence_records.line_range: unknown — source not directly available in grep_results; requires file fetch
evidence_records.extracted_content: Cipher.getInstance("AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding") — confirmed via preliminary_call_sites in Agent 1 structural_scan
evidence_records.explanation: Agent 1 confirmed this call site from the grep hits. Full method body including IV generation line (IvParameterSpec, SecureRandom) must be retrieved for complete labeling per prompt mandatory full method body rule.

Five JCA crypto imports present in EncryptionUtil. Confirms this file contains the full encryption/KDF implementation. IvParameterSpec import confirms IV parameter is explicitly constructed — source (static/random/derived) unknown without method body.
Agent 1 confirmed this call site. PBKDF2 with HMAC-SHA1 base is confirmed. Iteration count and key length are not visible without full method body — PBEKeySpec constructor arguments are required.

Agent 1 confirmed both call sites. BCrypt is confirmed as the password hashing algorithm for user authentication. Work factor (cost parameter) cannot be determined without inspecting the BCryptPasswordEncoder constructor invocation.
Both BCrypt-specific and generic PasswordEncoder imports confirmed. File implements PasswordEncoder interface using BCryptPasswordEncoder.

evidence_records.extracted_content: Interface PlatformPasswordEncoder — algorithm unknown, interface only
evidence_records.explanation: Agent 1 explicitly flags this as interface-only. Concrete algorithm requires implementor inspection. Known implementor: DefaultPlatformPasswordEncoder (BCrypt). Additional implementors may exist and must be searched.

evidence_records.extracted_content: DatabasePasswordEncryptor — delegates to EncryptionUtil per Agent 1 SIG-005 notes
evidence_records.explanation: Agent 1 confirms delegation pattern. The exact delegation method call must be confirmed from source to verify AES/PBKDF2 chain is correctly invoked.

evidence_records.extracted_content: DatabasePasswordDecryptor — delegates to EncryptionUtil per Agent 1 SIG-006 notes
evidence_records.explanation: Symmetric counterpart to DatabasePasswordEncryptor. IV handling on decryption (IV prepended vs. stored separately) is unknown and critical for correct AES/CBC labeling.

Agent 1 confirmed JWTParser.parse() call site. JWT parsing confirmed. Signature algorithm (RS256/HS256/ES256) cannot be inferred from parse() alone — algorithm is determined by JWK Set URI or symmetric secret configuration, not the parser.

Nimbus JOSE+JWT and Spring Security OAuth2 JWT imports confirm JWT processing. Algorithm resolution requires Spring Security JwtDecoder bean configuration, likely in AuthorizationServerConfig or SecurityConfig.

SecureRandom.nextBytes() appears in Agent 1 call_sites list. Attribution to RandomOTPGenerator is inferred — confirmation requires source inspection. java.security.SecureRandom import must be verified.

evidence_records.extracted_content: keystore.jks — JKS format binary; keytool inspection required for key type, size, validity, issuer
evidence_records.explanation: Agent 1 evidence_quality is test_code (file is in main/resources but may be a test/dev certificate). Binary artifact — content cannot be extracted without keytool or programmatic JKS parsing. Known limitations: algorithm, key size, expiry, issuer all unknown.

evidence_records.extracted_content: Liquibase XML changelog — tenant password encryption migration; specific SQL/changeSet content unknown without file fetch
evidence_records.explanation: Agent 1 SIG-014 confirms this file exists and relates to AES encryption of tenant passwords. The changeSet may invoke Java-side TenantPasswordEncryptionTask or embed inline SQL. Content must be fetched for labeling.

MessageDigest import in the structural_scan preliminary_imports indicates SHA-family or MD5 usage somewhere in the codebase. HashingService is the most likely source given class naming, but direct attribution is inferred.

evidence_records.explanation: Agent 1 explicitly classifies RSA in this file as a domain acronym. Classification as not_crypto pending source confirmation of no javax.crypto or java.security.interfaces.RSAKey imports.

evidence_records.extracted_content: tls — business term in method/variable name, not transport security config, per Agent 1 FP-002
evidence_records.explanation: Agent 1 explicitly classifies tls here as a business term. Classification as not_crypto pending source confirmation of no javax.net.ssl imports or TLS socket factory configuration.

evidence_records.extracted_content: PasswordValidationPolicy — password complexity/length policy enforcement, per Agent 1 FP-003
evidence_records.explanation: Agent 1 classifies this as policy rules only (not cryptographic). Classification as not_crypto pending confirmation of no PasswordEncoder or MessageDigest usage.

evidence_records.extracted_content: key — Java Map key or enum identifier, per Agent 1 FP-004
evidence_records.explanation: Agent 1 classifies 'key' in mapper files as data structure keys, not cryptographic key material. Classification as not_crypto pending confirmation.
